rationale: The "drop + satisfying chain reaction" hook structurally maps onto Marble Sort!'s tap-to-release-and-drop loop and targets the same female-skewing casual satisfaction audience, so the emotional pitch translates well. However, there are real frictions: the archetype shows MERGE mechanics (Suika-style) not SORT mechanics, which risks misleading installs and lower D1 retention, and the warm berry/cream/brown palette clashes with the game's soft lavender + orange identity. Recommend adapting the format—keep the drop-and-satisfying-payoff beat but swap fruit-merging for marbles cascading into matching color boxes, and re-skin to the game's purple/orange palette before testing.
